text:
Thank you very much. You be seated. It just makes my heart feel real good to be back in New York tonight. I've always got such a thrill of coming to New York. I like all of the states, but there's just something about New York that seems to grip me, and the people and their hospitality. Being a Southerner, I say you have to come up here in the north to find what Southern hospitality means. Come to the North.
I wish to take this time to thank Mr. Lloyd Sweet and all those who are affiliated with the Harvest Evangelism, the World Harvest Evangelism, for their kindness of sponsoring us through this New England campaign. And we are very grateful for all they have done and trust that the good Lord of heaven will just pour out His blessings upon the efforts and send the harvest of souls into the kingdom of God in their efforts.
text_no:
Tusen takk. Dere kan sette dere. Det gjør meg virkelig godt å være tilbake i New York i kveld. Jeg har alltid følt en spesiell glede ved å komme til New York. Jeg liker alle statene, men det er noe spesielt med New York som fanger meg, både menneskene og deres gjestfrihet. Som sørlending må jeg si at du må komme opp hit nord for å virkelig forstå hva sørlig gjestfrihet betyr. Kom til Nord.
Jeg ønsker å benytte anledningen til å takke Mr. Lloyd Sweet og alle som er tilknyttet Harvest Evangelism, World Harvest Evangelism, for deres vennlighet ved å sponse oss gjennom denne kampanjen i New England. Vi er veldig takknemlige for alt de har gjort, og vi stoler på at Herren i himmelen vil velsigne deres innsats rikelig og sende sjelers høst inn i Guds rike gjennom deres arbeid.

text: And I want to thank these ministers, tonight, here in New York City. I come to the platform, and the chairs were lined with ministers that's cooperating ministers. Thank you, my brethren, for dismissing your churches and coming out here to help make this a great rally. I sure appreciate it and trust that God will richly bless you in every way in your ministry. Hoping some time… I've always wanted a time to when we could set a great rally in New York, and just the Full Gospel people all get together, like the Baptists and those did for Billy Graham, and just have a great rally with all the people, the Spanish, and everyone together. Why, we could do that, and God would just rain down His blessings upon us, I'm sure, if we would just do that.
text_no:
Jeg vil takke disse forkynnerne her i kveld i New York City. Da jeg kom opp på scenen, var stolene fylt med forkynnere som samarbeider om dette. Takk, mine brødre, for at dere avlyste møtene deres og kom hit for å bidra til at dette blir et stort stevne. Jeg setter virkelig pris på det og stoler på at Gud vil velsigne dere rikt i deres tjeneste.
Jeg har alltid ønsket å arrangere et stort stevne i New York der alle i Full Gospel-miljøet samles, slik som baptistene gjorde for Billy Graham. Vi kunne få til et stort stevne med alle, inkludert spansktalende, samlet på ett sted. Gud ville sikkert la Sine velsignelser regne over oss hvis vi bare gjør det.

text:
And now, I'm certainly grateful for this evening. I'm tired. I've been preaching and having healing services about every day and night---not every day, but every night and some of the days, since I left. About the longest I ever went in one time and I'm really tired. Just a little hoarse, but very happy in my heart to report to you that there has been great things accomplished for our dear Lord in these New England states.
Just to give you a little view, if some of the brethren hasn't spoke of it: I was in the meeting one night when a lady died, sitting over to my left. And a doctor, there, went to find pulse in her and it was gone. To see the great Holy Spirit turn and call that woman's name and wake her up again… That happened in Hartford, Connecticut. And just things like that, that He's been doing.
text_no:
Jeg er virkelig takknemlig for denne kvelden. Jeg er sliten. Jeg har forkynt og hatt helbredelsesmøter nesten hver dag og kveld—ikke hver dag, men hver kveld og noen av dagene, siden jeg dro. Dette er den lengste perioden jeg har vært på én gang, og jeg er virkelig sliten. Litt hes, men veldig glad i hjertet over å kunne rapportere til dere at store ting har blitt oppnådd for vår kjære Herre i de nordøstlige statene.
For å gi dere et lite innblikk, i tilfelle noen av brødrene ikke har nevnt det: En kveld var jeg på et møte da en dame døde til venstre for meg. En lege der forsøkte å finne pulsen hennes, men den var borte. Å se Den Hellige Ånd snu seg, kalle damens navn og vekke henne til live igjen… Det skjedde i Hartford, Connecticut. Slike ting har Han gjort.

text:
Then it was up in Burlington, New Hampshire, where there was a lady… This is just one of the cases. She was sitting back, and the dear soul, she couldn't get a prayer card to get in the line, so she just bowed her humble little head and started praying. I believe she was an epileptic. And the Lord Jesus, with His grace, moved over there and told her that she was healed of this epilepsy. And said, "Also, your husband is given up in some hospital," that he has a disease that the doctors cannot cure. There's nothing can be done and they've give him up, but said, "Don't worry, for 'Thus saith the Lord,' he's just been healed." And the epilepsy left her immediately; and when she got home, the next morning, her husband called her. The doctors had taken him in for a final and could find nothing of it at all. He was on his road home, just rejoicing.
Oh, we have such a lovely Father, don't we? He's so good and we just love Him so much.
text_no:
Det var oppe i Burlington, New Hampshire, hvor det var en kvinne… Dette er bare ett av tilfellene. Hun satt bakerst, og den kjære sjelen kunne ikke få en bønnekort for å komme inn i køen, så hun bøyde bare sitt ydmyke lille hode og begynte å be. Jeg tror hun hadde epilepsi. Og Herren Jesus, i Sin nåde, kom over til henne og fortalte at hun var helbredet fra denne epilepsien. Han sa også: "Din ektemann er gitt opp på et sykehus," at han hadde en sykdom som legene ikke kunne kurere. Det var ingenting som kunne gjøres, og de hadde gitt ham opp, men sa: "Ikke bekymre deg, for 'Så sier Herren,' han er nettopp blitt helbredet." Epilepsien forlot henne umiddelbart; og da hun kom hjem neste morgen, ringte ektemannen hennes. Legene hadde tatt ham inn for en siste sjekk og kunne ikke finne noe feil i det hele tatt. Han var på vei hjem, bare jublende.
Å, vi har en så elskelig Far, har vi ikke? Han er så god, og vi elsker Ham så mye.

text:
And now, it's good to be here tonight, and all this fine fellowship. And some of our friends, I got to meet today: Captain Julius Stadsklev, all the way from the Mohammed desert … or, Mojave desert in California. He's a chaplain in the army, that was with me in Africa, that wrote the books. And he's here somewhere, tonight. And then also, the pastor Boze is here from Chicago, from the Philadelphian church. And just many good friends.
And I wonder if Mrs. Isaacson is here tonight? She was here the last time; I just got to wave at her when she left the platform. She's my voice of Finland. And I don't know whether… Are you here, Mrs. Isaacson? Raise up your hand, if you are. She come off the platform the last evening I was here, and I didn't get to see her. And she's a lovely person.
And just a Mr. Cox called me from over in Indiana, up around Hammond, is up here tonight. And we're happy for all of you, just very happy to have this time of fellowship. And God bless you.
text_no:
Det er godt å være her i kveld, sammen med alle dere fine mennesker. Jeg fikk møte noen av våre venner i dag: Kaptein Julius Stadsklev, som har kommet hele veien fra Mojave-ørkenen i California. Han er feltprest i hæren og var med meg i Afrika, hvor han skrev bøkene. Han er her i kveld et sted. 
Pastor Boze fra Philadelphia-menigheten i Chicago er også her. Mange gode venner er til stede. 
Er fru Isaacson her i kveld? Hun var her sist gang. Jeg fikk bare vinket til henne da hun forlot plattformen. Hun er stemmen min i Finland. Fru Isaacson, er du her? Løft hånden din hvis du er her. Hun gikk av plattformen sist jeg var her, og jeg fikk ikke sett henne. Hun er en fantastisk person.
Jeg fikk også en telefon fra Mr. Cox i Indiana, nær Hammond. Han er her i kveld. Vi er glade for å ha dere alle her og setter stor pris på fellesskapet. Gud velsigne dere.

text:
And Billy told me, as I come out the door just now, he said, "Daddy, they taken you up a love offering tonight." Now, I wasn't expecting that, friends. But if you did it, it's already… I couldn't give it back now, because I wouldn't know how to give it back. But thank you real kindly. And I will assure you, by the grace of God, I will spend every cent of it to the kingdom of God, the best of my knowledge to do that. Thank you kindly. And I just trust that God will repay you a hundredfold for every bit of it.
Now, when we leave here, we're on our road, next Sunday---not this coming Sunday; the following Sunday---at Dallas, Texas, in a big convention. Then from there we go up to North … or, South Carolina, Greenville, South Carolina: Interdenominational Ministerial Association Convention.
And then from there we're going to Green Pines, North Carolina, in a Baptist rally. And then from there, right back over to Philadelphia here, in the Full Gospel Christian Businessmen's convention. And we're just scattering around, trying to do what we can, 'cause we believe that the Lord is coming soon, and we want to do everything we can while it's daylight and we can work.
And in appreciation of all that's been done, I praise God for every bit of it, who has made it possible that these things could be done.
text_no:
Da jeg kom ut døren nå nettopp, sa Billy til meg: "Pappa, de har tatt opp en kjærlighetsgave til deg i kveld." Dette var ikke noe jeg forventet, venner. Men hvis dere har gjort det, kan jeg ikke gi det tilbake nå, fordi jeg ikke vet hvordan jeg skulle gå frem. Tusen takk. Jeg forsikrer dere, ved Guds nåde, at jeg vil bruke hver eneste krone på Guds rike, etter beste evne. Takk igjen. Jeg stoler på at Gud vil belønne dere hundrefold for hver bit av det.
Når vi reiser herfra, går turen til Dallas, Texas, neste søndag—ikke denne kommende søndagen, men den påfølgende—til et stort stevne. Deretter reiser vi til Greenville, South Carolina, for Interdenominational Ministerial Association Convention.
Etter det skal vi til Green Pines, North Carolina, for et baptistsstevne. Derfra går turen tilbake til Philadelphia for Full Gospel Christian Businessmen's stevne. Vi reiser mye rundt og gjør det vi kan, fordi vi tror Herren kommer snart, og vi vil arbeide mens det ennå er dagslys.
I takknemlighet for alt som er gjort, priser jeg Gud for hver eneste del, og for alle som har gjort det mulig at disse tingene kunne bli gjennomført.

text:
Now, just before we read His Word, let us bow our heads and speak to Him, just a moment.
Blessed Lord, when I think of Your goodness, and the souls that has been saved in these past few days, the thousands that has come to the meeting and been helped, I just forget about being tired. And then to be here tonight and to feel Your Spirit moving in these people, knowing that they're Your beloved, blood-washed people. And I thank you for each and every one of them. And I would pray, God, tonight, that You would just do something special for us. We're sitting here, under great anticipation. We're waiting and longing for that hour when the veil will be pulled back, and we shall see Him.
text_no:
Nå, rett før vi leser Hans Ord, la oss bøye våre hoder og snakke med Ham et øyeblikk.
Velsignede Herre, når jeg tenker på Din godhet og de sjelene som har blitt frelst de siste dagene, de tusenvis som har kommet til møtene og fått hjelp, glemmer jeg helt at jeg er trøtt. Og så å være her i kveld og føle Din Ånd bevege seg blant disse menneskene, vel vitende om at de er Dine elskede, blodvaskede mennesker. Jeg takker Deg for hver og en av dem. Jeg ber Deg, Gud, at Du i kveld vil gjøre noe spesielt for oss. Vi sitter her i stor forventning. Vi venter og lengter etter den stund når sløret skal trekkes tilbake, og vi skal se Ham.

text:
And how we love Him and would love to fall on our faces and just touch His feet, for we love Him. And Father, I pray that You'll bless every person here, and every minister, every pastor, evangelist, missionary, whatever. Grant that their churches will prosper, and their work for the Lord will increase, and many souls may be saved, and something might be done, tonight, that would help us to take a new hold and start new again. Grant it, Father.
Now, we have opened up the lid of Your Bible. And now, interpret for us, Father. May the Holy Spirit speak in such a way, tonight, that when we go to our different places, may we say like those who came from Emmaus, "Did not our hearts burn within us as He talked to us along the way," for we ask it in Jesus' name, Thy Son, Amen.
text_no:
Og hvordan vi elsker Ham og ville ønske å falle på våre ansikter og bare berøre Hans føtter, for vi elsker Ham. Og Far, jeg ber om at Du vil velsigne hver person her, og enhver forkynner, pastor, evangelist, misjonær, uansett. Gi at menighetene deres vil blomstre, og deres arbeid for Herren vil øke, og at mange sjeler blir frelst. La noe skje i kveld som hjelper oss å få et nytt grep og starte på nytt igjen. Gi oss det, Far.
Nå har vi åpnet lokket på Din Bibel. Og nå, tolke for oss, Far. Måtte Den Hellige Ånd tale på en slik måte i kveld at når vi går til våre forskjellige steder, kan vi si som de som kom fra Emmaus: "Brant ikke våre hjerter i oss da Han talte til oss på veien," for vi ber i Jesu navn, Din Sønn, Amen.

text:
I wish to read tonight, being that the meeting is just a little on it's way now… Not to take too much time---we got around a thousand miles to drive right away. And I want to read from the book of Saint Mark, the 12th chapter … the 11th chapter, rather, of Saint Mark and the 22nd verse.
And Jesus, answering said unto them, Have faith in God.
This little word "faith," have faith in God… And for a subject, I would like to use this: Time Tested Faith, those three words, Time Tested Faith. It's just three words, but oh, what it means.
text_no:
I kveld ønsker jeg å lese fra Bibelen, nå som møtet er godt i gang. For å ikke ta for mye tid, må vi kjøre omtrent 1600 kilometer straks etterpå. Jeg vil lese fra Markus-evangeliet, kapittel 11, vers 22.
Og Jesus svarte og sa til dem: "Ha tro til Gud."
Dette lille ordet "tro," ha tro til Gud… Som tema ønsker jeg å bruke dette: Tidsprøvd Tro, de tre ordene, Tidsprøvd Tro. Det er bare tre ord, men å, hva de betyr.

text:
And I am told in the Old Testament, that faith, in the Old Testament, was the same way it is in the New Testament. So many people stumble at the thought of faith. But it's so simple to have faith.
For instance, you cannot move out of your seat, if you were able to come in and sit down, you cannot move from your seat unless you have faith that you'll do it. You cannot put your hat on again unless you have faith you can do it. Never can you draw another breath unless you have faith you can do it.
Faith is so common, that when we look at it for something a little unusual, we go way over the top of it, trying to find it's what's right next to us.
text_no:
Jeg har blitt fortalt at tro i Det gamle testamentet er det samme som i Det nye testamentet. Mange snubler ved tanken på tro, men det er så enkelt å ha tro.
For eksempel kan du ikke reise deg fra stolen hvis du ikke har tro på at du kan gjøre det. Du kan ikke ta på deg hatten igjen uten tro på at du klarer det. Du kan heller ikke trekke neste åndedrag uten tro på at det er mulig.
Tro er så vanlig at når vi ser etter noe litt uvanlig, overser vi det som er rett foran oss.

text:
Now, in the Old Testament when the blood was applied over the lintels of the door, I'm told it was put on by hyssop dipped in the blood and rubbed over the door. Hyssop is just common weed. And that's how it was applied, by hyssop, something you could just reach down anywhere and get a handful of it.
And how appropriate that is, tonight, to apply hyssop as faith. You put the blood on by faith. How we're covered by the blood: by faith, common faith.
Well, you say, "But for miracles and for healing, Brother Branham, is some different type of faith." No, it's the same faith. There's only one faith. And all good things, and all things that are, are original.
text_no:
I Det Gamle Testamentet ble blodet påført dørkarmene ved hjelp av isop, som ble dyppet i blodet og smurt over døren. Isop er en vanlig ugressplante. Dette er en passende parallell, fordi vi i dag påfører blodet ved hjelp av tro. Vi blir dekket av blodet gjennom tro, en enkel, vanlig tro.
Du sier kanskje, «Men, for mirakler og for helbredelse, Bror Branham, trengs en annen type tro.» Nei, det er den samme troen. Det finnes kun én tro. Og alle gode ting, alt som er, er originale.

text:
All evil is just righteousness perverted. Sickness is health perverted. And everything that's wrong, is right being perverted. Well, what perverted it was Satan. And Jesus died that He might give us the power, through His blood, to correct that thing that's wrong: bring sinners back to Salvation, sickness back to health, unrighteousness back to righteousness. See, it's just that simple.
And many times we go over it, looking to see, "Hunting, oh, if I could only do this…" You could if you'd just don't be excited about it.
text_no:
Alle onde handlinger er rettferdighet som er forvrengt. Sykdom er helse som er forvrengt. Alt som er galt, er rett som er blitt forvrengt. Det var Satan som forvrengte dette. Jesus døde for å gi oss makt, gjennom Sitt blod, til å rette opp det som er galt: bringe syndere tilbake til frelse, sykdom tilbake til helse, og urettferdighet tilbake til rettferdighet. Se, det er så enkelt.
Ofte overser vi dette mens vi tenker, "Å, hvis jeg bare kunne gjøre dette…" Du kunne, hvis du bare ikke ble oppjaget over det.

text:
As I just was hearing Dr. Vayle as I was leaving, saying that my ministry was changing… Oh, I'm so happy. Not this isn't going to leave; it'll remain, always. But it's going to be something greater.
Last evening, when we were going from the building, after a little child had been so very disturbing, mentally gone, and the little mother could not even hold it in the building; it was just kicking and screaming and, oh, carrying on awful. And she'd take it outside, and I could hear it way down the hall. And then after this… The Lord had did great things. On the road out, there the little mother was holding that poor little mental baby; it's little eyes, almost out on it's cheeks; it's little mouth drawn sideways; and it was kicking and screaming. And when I come up, it just went into a tantrum. But oh, that blessed something, the presence of the Holy Spirit… I said to Mr. Vayle and to Billy, who were taking me out, I said, "Here is the new coming." And caught the little fellow by the arm and said, "Dear God, let it return normal." And it stopped, and looked up at me, and smiled, and the mother picked it up and left the building.
text_no:
Da jeg nettopp hørte på Dr. Vayle mens jeg var på vei ut, og han sa at tjenesten min var i ferd med å endre seg... Å, jeg er så glad. Dette vil ikke forsvinne; det vil alltid forbli. Men det vil bli noe større.
I går kveld, da vi forlot bygningen, etter at et lite barn hadde vært svært forstyrrende, mentalt borte, og den lille moren ikke engang kunne holde det i bygningen; barnet sparket og skrek og bar seg forferdelig. Hun tok barnet med ut, og jeg kunne høre det langt nede i hallen. Etter dette... Herren hadde gjort store ting. På veien ut sto den lille moren og holdt det stakkars lille sinnsforstyrrede barnet; dets øyne nesten ute på kinnene, munnen trukket sidelengs, og det sparket og skrek. Da jeg kom nærmere, fikk barnet et raserianfall. Men å, den velsignede tilstedeværelsen av Den Hellige Ånd... Jeg sa til Mr. Vayle og Billy, som fulgte meg ut, "Her kommer det nye." Jeg tok tak i barnets arm og sa, "Kjære Gud, la det bli normalt." Barnet stoppet, så opp på meg og smilte, og moren løftet det opp og forlot bygningen.

text:
In our Scripture tonight, the previous chapters, Jesus had said to a tree, "No fruit grow on thee … or, no man eateth from thee." Then He said, after this verse, "If you say to this mountain be moved…" Not "If I say,"; "If you say to this mountain be moved…"
Now, we know that man could not move a mountain just by speaking to it. The only thing could do that would be Deity. So then, if the mountain is before us, and our objective is right, and our motive is right, then it isn't us speaking any more; it's Deity speaking. Then it has to move.
I truly believe that the church is on the brink of one of the greatest outpourings that it's ever had in this age. I believe it. I can't say the Lord has told me, but it's just something inside me, just catching a hold of something, that I haven't seen all my life till just now.
And how glorious it is to know, just before the coming of the Lord, that these things are happening.
text_no:
I kveldens Skriftsteder ser vi at Jesus i de foregående kapitlene hadde sagt til et tre: "Ingen spiser frukt fra deg igjen." Deretter sier Han: "Hvis dere sier til dette fjellet, 'Flytt deg'…" Ikke "Hvis Jeg sier"; "Hvis dere sier til dette fjellet, 'Flytt deg'…"
Vi vet at et menneske ikke kan flytte et fjell bare ved å tale til det. Den eneste som kan gjøre det, er Guddommen. Derfor, hvis fjellet er foran oss, og vårt mål og vår motivasjon er rette, er det ikke lenger oss som taler; det er Guddommen som taler. Da må fjellet flytte seg.
Jeg tror virkelig at menigheten er på grensen til en av de største utgytelsene den noensinne har opplevd i denne tidsalderen. Jeg tror det. Jeg kan ikke si at Herren har sagt det til meg, men det er noe inni meg som griper fatt i noe jeg ikke har sett hele mitt liv før nå.
Hvor herlig er det ikke å vite at disse tingene skjer rett før Herrens komme.

text:
Now, faith is so simple. The Scripture says, "Faith is the substance of things hoped for, the evidence of things not seen."
Now, faith is not a myth. It's something that you actually possess, just like this: if I had a… If I was starving, and you come to me, and I said, "I'm starving."
You say, "What would save your life?"
I'd say, "A loaf of bread."
And you'd go into your pocket and bring out twenty-five cents and hand it to me and say, "Here you are, Mr. Branham, you don't have to starve."
Well, I haven't got the bread yet, but I've got the purchase power of the bread. Now, I may be a mile from the bread, but I can be just as happy with that quarter in my hand, as I can when I got the bread in my hand, because I've got the purchase power of the bread.
And when faith is anchored, that it's finished, you're just as happy as if you was already jumping and shouting, because it's all over. You have it. It's something you know you've got. Nothing can move it. You never watch for any evidences or anything else; you just know you have it.
text_no:
Tro er så enkelt. Skriften sier: "Tro er full visshet om det man håper, overbevisning om ting en ikke ser." Tro er ikke en myte; det er noe du faktisk besitter. For eksempel, hvis jeg var sulten og du kom til meg, og jeg sa: "Jeg er sulten."
Du spør: "Hva ville redde livet ditt?"
Jeg ville svart: "En brødskive."
Så tar du fram tjuefem øre fra lomma og gir dem til meg og sier: "Her, Mr. Branham, du trenger ikke å sulte."
Vel, jeg har ikke brødet ennå, men jeg har kjøpekraften til å få brødet. Selv om jeg er en kilometer unna brødet, kan jeg være like glad med den tjuefem-øringen i hånden som om jeg hadde brødet i hånden, for jeg har kjøpekraften til brødet.
Når troen er forankret og det er fullført, er du like glad som om du allerede hopper og roper, fordi alt er over. Du har det. Det er noe du vet du har. Ingenting kan rykke det. Du ser aldri etter noen bevis eller noe annet; du bare vet at du har det.

text:
Now, when I got this bread (this twenty-five cents), I may have to go over some hills, go through a briar patch, across some water, and might have to cross bridges, but all the time I'm rejoicing, because I've got the twenty-five cents, knowing I'm going to get the bread just as soon as I get there. It's the purchase power. Well, faith is the purchase power of things you've hoped for. That is genuine faith. Nothing can move it.
Now, the most people have faith but they just don't try to use what faith they've got. I believe that every person that believes on the Lord Jesus Christ has to have a certain amount of faith. But the reason that it's never used too much, it's because you never put it to use very much. A person usually coming to church, will just come in, and listen to a sermon, and then they'll go up, and put their name on the church book, and become a good member of that church. That's just about as far as they ever have the opportunity to use their faith.
text_no:
Når jeg har fått tak i dette brødet (dette tjuefem ørene), kan det hende jeg må krysse noen åser, gå gjennom et tornekratt, over litt vann og kanskje krysse noen broer, men hele tiden gleder jeg meg, fordi jeg har tjuefem øre og vet at jeg kommer til å få brødet så snart jeg kommer frem. Det er kjøpekraften. Vel, tro er kjøpekraften for det du har håpet på. Det er ekte tro. Ingenting kan rokke ved den.
De fleste har tro, men de prøver bare ikke å bruke den troen de har. Jeg tror at hver person som tror på Herren Jesus Kristus må ha en viss mengde tro. Grunnen til at den ikke blir brukt så mye, er at den ikke blir brukt ofte nok. En person som vanligvis går i menigheten, kommer inn, lytter til en preken, skriver navnet sitt i menighetsboken og blir et godt medlem. Det er omtrent så langt de får mulighet til å bruke sin tro.

text:
Now, faith will just do anything that you have a need of being done, if you'll put it to work. But most churches today, they got just as far as they teach: just accept Jesus by faith and that settles it.
That's like a man was that was eating a slice of watermelon. He said, "That was good, but there's some more of it somewhere." So that's the way it is: to accept Christ is good, but there's more of it.
And then, when a man accepts Christ as his personal Saviour, then all the good things that God has promised is laying right at his finger tips---just to believe it.
Now, don't go at it in a place where you're just, "I wonder if it'll work." Just the same as you know you're going to get a drink of water, you're going to raise up and walk across the floor; that's just how simple to be about it. Well, the Lord promised it, that settles it, and just go on about it. That's real genuine faith.
text_no:
Tro kan utrette alt du trenger, hvis du bruker den riktig. Men de fleste menigheter i dag lærer bare så langt som å akseptere Jesus ved tro, og mener dermed at alt er ordnet.
Det er som en mann som spiste en skive vannmelon og sa: "Det var godt, men det er mer et sted." På samme måte er det å akseptere Kristus godt, men det er mer å hente.
Når en mann aksepterer Kristus som sin personlige Frelser, ligger alle de gode tingene Gud har lovet rett foran ham—bare å tro på det.
Ikke tilnærm deg det med tvil som: "Jeg lurer på om det vil fungere." Akkurat som du vet at du skal drikke vann eller reise deg og gå over gulvet, skal du være like enkel i din tro. Herren har lovet det, det avgjør saken, og gå videre med det. Dette er ekte, genuin tro.

text:
Now, if people would put… If the church would put more faith to work, you'd see more things taking place in the church. The pastor might stand on Sunday morning, he might stand on Sunday evening or whenever, and preach hard on faith, in a mighty message, but if the members don't take that message and then start putting it to work, it'll fall right back, and the birds of the air will pick it up, see, see. Just take it out and put it to work. God wants you to do it.
Now, faith… Always an experience accompanies faith. And how could it be tonight? I'd say to you women here, that you've got little canary birds and so forth that you love, little pets. What good would it do for you to give them all the vitamins that you can in their seed, to make big strong bones, and fine wings, and so forth, if you keep him in the cage all the time? See, there's no need of giving him vitamins, if you're going to keep him in a cage. He don't need the vitamins to make strong wings, because he's never got a chance to use them.
text_no:
Hvis folk hadde satt mer tro ut i handling, ville man se flere ting skje i menigheten. Pastoren kunne stå på søndag morgen, søndag kveld eller når som helst og forkynne kraftig om tro i en mektig tale. Men hvis medlemmene ikke tar budskapet og begynner å sette det ut i livet, vil det falle til bakken, og luftens fugler vil plukke det opp. Ta det ut og sett det i arbeid. Gud ønsker at du skal gjøre det.
Tro ledsages alltid av erfaring. Hvordan kunne det være i kveld? Jeg vil si til dere kvinner her, som har små kanarifugler og andre kjæledyr dere er glad i: Hva godt gjør det å gi dem alle vitaminene de kan få i frøene sine, for å få store, sterke bein og fine vinger, hvis dere holder dem i bur hele tiden? Det er unyttig å gi dem vitaminer hvis de skal forbli i et bur. De trenger ikke sterke vinger, fordi de aldri får sjansen til å bruke dem.

text:
What good does it do to preach a Bible, of a God that heals, or the God that's got power, and tell the people that the days of miracles is past? What good does it do to send ministers to seminaries, and to colleges, and so forth, and learn all these different things, and then come tell the people that's something that was; it isn't any more?
It's just like taking a man, freezing to death, and say, "Here's a nice picture of a big painted fire." The man cannot get warm by a painted fire. He's freezing and he's got to have warmth.
And a man that's thirsting for God can't be satisfied till something happens. It's an experience. And that kind of an experience will bring forth great faith. You never know what it is. You cannot trust God who you have never met.
text_no:
Hva godt gjør det å forkynne en Bibel om en Gud som helbreder, eller en Gud med makt, samtidig som man forteller folk at miraklenes tid er forbi? Hva godt gjør det å sende forkynnere til seminarer og høyskoler for å lære dem forskjellige ting, bare for å komme tilbake og fortelle folk at det var noe som var, men ikke er mer?
Det er som å vise en frysende mann et fint bilde av et stort, malt bål. Mannen kan ikke bli varm av et malt bål; han fryser og trenger ekte varme.
En mann som tørster etter Gud kan ikke bli tilfreds før noe skjer. Det er en erfaring. Og en slik erfaring vil bringe frem stor tro. Du vet aldri hva det er. Du kan ikke stole på en Gud du aldri har møtt.

text:
For instance, one time in the Bible, Israel was called out in war against the Philistines. And they were standing over on a hill, and Israel had gathered themselves across the valley over on the other side. And of course, just like the devil, when he thinks he's got the bluff on you, he will just call your hand to anything.
And they had a great big, prehistoric, giant over there by the name of Goliath. His fingers was fourteen inches long, and he had a spear like a weavers needle, perhaps, from here to the steps. And he's made a proposition with the people of Israel. He said (how cunning sin is), he said, "Let's not have any bloodshed now." He said, "There's no need of everybody getting all butchered up in this war. You look you out a man over there and let him come over here and fight me. And if I whip him, you serve us. And if he whips me, then we will serve you."
Now, that's the way the devil does it when he thinks he's got the upper hand.
text_no:
En gang i Bibelen ble Israel kalt ut til krig mot filisterne. Israel sto på en ås, mens filisterne hadde samlet seg på den andre siden av dalen. Som typisk for djevelen, når han tror han har overtaket, utfordrer han deg uten å blunke.
De hadde en stor, forhistorisk kjempe ved navn Goliat. Han hadde fjorten tommer lange fingre og et spyd så stort som en vevers pinner. Han kom med et forslag til Israels folk, og sa, "La oss unngå blodbadet. Det er ikke nødvendig at alle skal slaktes i denne krigen. Velg ut en mann til å kjempe mot meg. Hvis jeg slår ham, skal dere tjene oss. Hvis han slår meg, skal vi tjene dere."
Slik opererer djevelen når han tror han har overtaket.

text:
If there was any man in the whole army of Israel that was able to fight this giant, was Saul. He was head and shoulders above his entire army. He was a warrior from his youth. He knowed how to handle a shield, a spear; how to maneuver that spear out of another man's hand. He'd taught men, for he was a bishop. He knowed how to teach all the theology. But the thing of it was, he never had had any experience of meeting God with it.
And this big giant come out every day and made his boast. But one day he made his boast once too often.
There was a little ruddy looking fellow, stoop-shouldered and hair hanging in his eyes, come up into the camp to visit his brethren. He had some raisin pie, and so forth, his Daddy had sent up there to the boys. And his name was David. He was a little sheep herder. And when he heard this giant come out there and said, "What you going to do about it, fellows?" He said it at the wrong time. It fell on the ears of somebody who knew God.
He said, "Do you mean to tell me that the armies of the living God will stand here and let that uncircumcised Philistine defy these armies?"
text_no:
Hvis det var noen i hele Israels hær som kunne kjempe mot denne kjempen, var det Saul. Han var høyere enn hele sin hær. Han var en kriger fra ungdommen. Han visste hvordan man håndterer et skjold og et spyd; hvordan man manøvrerer et spyd ut av en annen manns hånd. Han hadde undervist menn, for han var en biskop. Han kunne all teologien. Men problemet var at han aldri hadde hatt noen erfaring med å møte Gud ved det.
Denne store kjempen kom ut hver dag og skrøt av seg selv. Men en dag skrøt han litt for mye.
En liten rødmusset fyr, lutrygget og med håret hengende i øynene, kom inn i leiren for å besøke sine brødre. Han hadde med seg rosinkake og lignende, som hans far hadde sendt med ham til guttene. Hans navn var David. Han var en liten sauegjeter. Da han hørte kjempen komme ut og utfordre, spurte han: "Hva skal dere gjøre med dette, gutter?" Han spurte på feil tidspunkt. Det falt på ørene til noen som kjente Gud.
David sa: "Mener du å fortelle meg at hæren til den levende Gud vil stå her og la denne uomskårne filisteren utfordre disse hærene?"

text:
I wonder today, how it could be that the uncircumcised could raise up and stand in the midst of a day, when Jesus Christ has manifested Himself on earth with an open Bible. You mean that this uncircumcised world, of all of these theology and educations and so forth, that I'd stand still, when my God lives and defy God's Holy Word? Never! If I had to sink in my tracks, I would take God and stand on His Word. Why? These ministers will do the same thing. Why? The same reason David said what he did. Let them call them holy-rollers. Let them call them whatever they may call them. They know what they're talking about.
"Oh," you say, "But this fellow's an archbishop." He might be a pope, but that don't make any difference.
David didn't have all the training that Saul had had, and he was just a little bitty runt. But he was… The Bible said he was ruddy, just a little fellow. As the little sheepskin coat on a little, you know, like "a little panty-waist," we call them. And here he was standing there looking up at his brothers and them, saying, "You mean that you'll let that uncircumcised Philistine defy the armies of the living God?" Said, "I will go fight him."
Oh, that was a courage. God give us some more Davids, today. What we need.
text_no:
Jeg undrer meg i dag over hvordan det kan være at de uomskårne kan reise seg og stå i en tid som denne, når Jesus Kristus har åpenbart Seg på jorden med en åpen Bibel. Mener du at denne uomskårne verden, fylt med all denne teologi og utdannelse, skal stå stille når min Gud lever og trosser Guds Hellige Ord? Aldri! Selv om jeg måtte synke i mine spor, vil jeg ta Gud og stå på Hans Ord. Hvorfor? Disse forkynnerne vil gjøre det samme. Hvorfor? Av samme grunn som David gjorde det han gjorde. La dem kalle dem hellighetsskruller. La dem kalle dem hva de vil. De vet hva de snakker om.
"Å," sier du, "men denne fyren er en erkebiskop." Han kan være en pave, men det spiller ingen rolle.
David hadde ikke all opplæringen som Saul hadde, og han var bare en liten tufs. Men han var... Bibelen sier at han var rødlig, bare en liten fyr. Med en liten saueulljakke, kanskje litt som en "liten spjæling," som vi kaller dem. Og her sto han og så opp på sine brødre, og sa: "Mener dere at dere vil la den uomskårne filisteren håne den levende Guds hærer?" Sa, "Jeg vil gå og kjempe mot ham."
Åh, det var mot. Gud, gi oss flere Davider i dag. Det er det vi trenger.

text:
What was David talking from? And his brothers said, "Oh, I know you want to be naughty. We just know you come up here with that naughtiness in your voice, so we're going to send you back home."
And it got around to Saul. So Saul called him up and wanted to know who this stripling was. And they brought him up there in the presence of Saul, and Saul said, "You can't fight that man." Said, "You're nothing but a youth. And he's a warrior from his youth." And he said… I want you to notice what David said. Saul said, "Just a minute. If you want to go, I will fix you up." So he took his own shield off and handed it over to David and put his armor on David. And could you imagine what his helmet looked… That big head of Saul's and that hat sitting down over the top of little David's ears. And the shoulders about this wide and a shield of armor about that wide. Why the poor little fellow couldn't hold it up; that's trying to put doctor's degrees on a man of God.
text_no:
Hva snakket David om? Brødrene hans sa: "Å, vi vet at du vil være ulydig. Vi vet at du kommer hit med den ulydige tonen, så vi sender deg hjem igjen."
Dette nådde Saul, som kalte David til seg for å finne ut hvem denne ungdommen var. David ble ført inn for Saul, som sa: "Du kan ikke kjempe mot den mannen. Du er bare en ung gutt, og han har vært kriger siden ungdommen."
Jeg vil at dere skal legge merke til hva David sa. Saul sa, "Et øyeblikk. Hvis du vil gå, skal jeg utruste deg." Så tok Saul av seg sitt eget skjold og ga det til David. Han kledde også David i sin rustning. Kan du forestille deg hvordan hjelmen til Saul så ut på lille David? Den store hjelmen dekket nesten ørene hans, og skuldrebredden på rustningen var for stor. Den stakkars gutten klarte ikke å holde rustningen oppe; det er som å prøve å gi doktorgrader til en Guds mann.

text:
And Saul found out that his theological vest didn't fit a man of God. He said, "Take this thing off of me. I don't know nothing about it." How to stand up and repeat the apostle's creed, so called, and say, "Ahhh-men." Why, he didn't know nothing about that.
He said, "But let me go with something that I know what I'm talking about." God knows that's what we need tonight, is men in the army of God who knows what they're talking about. Not whether they have a doctor's degree or what; they might not know their ABC's, but they know Christ and that's the main thing.
David said, "I've had an experience. I had an experience of this power that I'm speaking of. One day I was back out there on the backside of the desert, herding my Daddy's sheep, and a bear run in and got one. And I just took the slingshot and knocked him down with it; took it out of his mouth." And said, "A lion came in and got one, and I knocked him down, and he raised up against me, and I slew him. Why," he said, "the God that delivered them out of my hands, won't He much more deliver that uncircumcised Philistine that's defying the armies of the living God?"
text_no:
Saul oppdaget at hans teologiske rustning ikke passet en mann av Gud. Han sa: "Ta denne av meg. Jeg vet ingenting om dette." Han visste ikke hvordan man skulle stå opp og gjenta apostlenes trosbekjennelse, og si "Amen." Han hadde ingen kjennskap til det.
Han sa: "Men la meg gå med noe jeg vet hva jeg snakker om." Gud vet at det vi trenger i kveld, er menn i Guds hær som vet hva de snakker om. Ikke nødvendigvis om de har en doktorgrad eller ikke; de trenger kanskje ikke kunne sitt ABC, men de kjenner Kristus, og det er det viktigste.
David sa: "Jeg har hatt en erfaring. Jeg har hatt en erfaring med denne kraften jeg snakker om. En dag var jeg der ute i ørkenens bakside og gjetet min Fars sauer, og en bjørn kom og tok en. Jeg brukte slyngen og felte den; tok sauen ut av munnen dens." Og han fortsatte: "En løve kom og tok en, og jeg felte ham, og han reiste seg imot meg, og jeg drepte ham. Gud som reddet dem fra mine hender, vil Han ikke mye mer redde den uomskårne filisteren som utfordrer den levende Guds hær?"

text:
If God delivered you from sin, how much more will He deliver you from your sickness. He's able to take and change your nature and make you a new person. Why would you fear about any sickness? And if He can save your soul and change your nature, change your motives, change your ideas, how much more can He change your sickness into health and put it back in it's right place again, where it belongs. It's just so simple that we go over the top of it.
And 'course he said, "I admire your courage, son, but it's your own death." So he took his little slingshot and got five rocks. Put one in, and Goliath made fun of him. I want you to watch. He had five rocks. And you boys know what an old slingshot is: a piece of leather and… Why, we used to be able to knock a snake doctor off the fence with it, just as easy as could be. And then we'd… That's five fingers, five rocks; F-a-i-t-h in J-e-s-u-s. Here he come. Giant's got to fall now. And he won the victory. Because why? He had an experience that he knowed that God could deliver.
text_no:
Hvis Gud har frigjort deg fra synd, hvor mye mer vil Han ikke da frigjøre deg fra din sykdom? Han er i stand til å ta og forandre din natur og gjøre deg til en ny person. Hvorfor skulle du da frykte enhver sykdom? Hvis Han kan frelse din sjel og forandre din natur, dine motiver og dine ideer, hvor mye mer kan Han ikke forvandle din sykdom til helse og gjenopprette deg til ditt rette sted. Det er så enkelt at vi ofte overser det.
Han sa: "Jeg beundrer ditt mot, sønn, men det er din egen død." Så tok han sin lille slyngeskudd og plukket opp fem steiner. Han la en i slyngen, og Goliat hånte ham. Jeg vil at du skal legge merke til dette: Han hadde fem steiner, og dere gutter vet hva en gammel slyngeskudd er: et stykke lær og... Vi pleide å kunne treffe en øyenstikker fra et gjerde med det, like enkelt som ingenting. Det var fem fingre, fem steiner; T-r-o i J-e-s-u-s. Der kom han. Kjempen må falle nå. Og han vant seieren. Hvorfor? Fordi han hadde en erfaring som viste ham at Gud kunne frigjøre.

text:
A man's not qualified to stand behind the platform or the pulpit, until He's met God and had an experience. No person has a right to call themselves a Christian, until first they've had an experience with God. That's right.
It was Abraham, after… He was just an ordinary man that came down and dwelt in Shinar, down in the city of Ur. And he was just an ordinary believer. But one day God met him, and He spoke to him. And Abraham had an experience. And after Abraham had that experience, he could tell Sarah, "Go buy the Birdseye and get all the pins ready; we're going to have the baby. I don't care how old you are."
Why? He had had an experience first, of talking face to face with God. He could call those things which were not as though they were, after he had met God and had an experience---not before it; after it.
That's what's the matter with people tonight, in these big cities and all cities in the world, is because they refuse to receive the Holy Spirit.
text_no:
En mann er ikke kvalifisert til å stå bak plattformen eller prekestolen før Han har møtt Gud og hatt en opplevelse. Ingen har rett til å kalle seg en kristen før de først har hatt en opplevelse med Gud. Det stemmer.
Abraham var bare en vanlig mann som kom ned og bodde i Shinar, i byen Ur. Han var en helt vanlig troende. Men en dag møtte Gud ham, og Han talte til ham. Abraham hadde en opplevelse. Etter at Abraham hadde hatt denne opplevelsen, kunne han si til Sara: "Gå og kjøp Birdseye og gjør alt klart; vi skal få en baby. Jeg bryr meg ikke om hvor gammel du er."
Hvorfor? Fordi han først hadde hatt en opplevelse av å snakke ansikt til ansikt med Gud. Han kunne kalle de ting som ikke var som om de var, etter at han hadde møtt Gud og hatt en opplevelse—ikke før det; etter det.
Det er problemet med folk i dag, i disse store byene og alle byene i verden, at de nekter å motta Den Hellige Ånd.

text:
You Methodists, and you Baptists, and you Presbyterian, and Catholic, you're human beings which Christ died for. And you've got just as much right to these blessings as the Pentecostals has. But the only thing it is, you'll just sign your name to a church book, instead of staying there until you've got an experience that God has changed you by the baptism of the Holy Ghost. It takes an experience to accompany faith. You know where you've been. Why, David knowed what he was talking about. He had met God.
Abraham knew what he was talking about, for he'd met God. For twenty-five years he endured, because he believed that God that he had met, took all shadows of doubt away from him. And he waited twenty-five years until that baby was born. Why? He had an experience of meeting God.
text_no:
Dere metodister, baptister, presbyterianere og katolikker, dere er mennesker som Kristus døde for. Dere har like mye rett til disse velsignelsene som pinsevennene. Men det eneste er at dere bare skriver navnet deres i en kirkebok, i stedet for å bli der til dere har fått en opplevelse hvor Gud har forandret dere ved Den Hellige Ånds dåp. Det kreves en opplevelse som følger med troen. Du vet hvor du har vært. David visste hva han snakket om. Han hadde møtt Gud.
Abraham visste hva han snakket om, for han hadde møtt Gud. Han holdt ut i tjuefem år fordi han trodde på Gud, som han hadde møtt, og kastet vekk alle skygger av tvil. Han ventet i tjuefem år til barnet ble født. Hvorfor? Fordi han hadde en opplevelse av å møte Gud.

text:
Now, it was Moses that was raised up in all of the theology that Egypt could afford to give him. He was so smart until he could teach the masters of Egypt some wisdom. And he knew that he was born a Hebrew. He knew that he was born a deliverer. But look what a mess he made out of it, when he tried within himself, before he had an experience. He had all the schooling. He had the knowledge that he was the deliverer. But you see, he had never had any experience yet. But oh, brother, when he met God there in that burning bush, he knowed what he was after then; he knowed who he had believed. He could endure the rest of the eighty years as seeing Him who is invisible.
What a ridiculous sight, to see a man… How faith, meeting God, will change a man. It'll make you do things that seem so crazy to the world. It'll make you act different, and you won't care what anybody says as long as you know what you've been talking to.
text_no:
Moses hadde fått all teologisk opplæring Egypt kunne tilby. Han var så klok at han kunne lære Egypts mestere visdom. Han visste at han var født som hebreer og at han var født som en befrier. Men se hvordan det gikk når han forsøkte i egen kraft, før han hadde en virkelig erfaring. Han hadde all utdanning og visste at han var befrieren. Men han hadde ennå ingen erfaring. Men, bror, da han møtte Gud i den brennende busken, visste han hva han skulle gjøre; han visste hvem han trodde på. Han kunne holde ut resten av de åtti årene som om han så Ham som er usynlig.
For en latterlig syn, å se en mann... hvordan troen, ved å møte Gud, kan forandre en mann. Det kan få deg til å gjøre ting som virker helt galne for verden. Det vil få deg til å oppføre deg annerledes, og du vil ikke bry deg om hva noen sier, så lenge du vet hvem du har snakket med.

text:
Moses, running from Egypt, forty years on the desert, but five minutes in the presence of that bush, the next morning here he goes down to Egypt, his wife sitting astraddle of a mule, with a young'un on each hip. And him a man eighty years old with an old stick in his hand, the whiskers a blowing. "Where you going, Moses?"
"Going down to Egypt to take over." A one man invasion. The thing of it was, he did it.
Why? He had an experience as a warrior and a young man. He run from the presence of Pharaoh without an experience. But with an experience, he walked into his face and told him, "I will smite this land with plagues, unless you let this people go." He wasn't afraid. Why? He'd met God face to face.
Brother, sister, there is a secret place that every believer should go: the backside of the desert, there on those sacred sands. Oh, no doctor of theology, no Greek or Hebrew scholar, no school or seminary… They might twist you all up in these kind of things, but if you ever met God on those sacred sands, Satan can't put his feet on them sands. You know you met God. Something real… What was it? You talked to God yourself. You had an experience.
text_no:
Moses, som rømte fra Egypten og tilbrakte førti år i ørkenen, endret seg dramatisk etter fem minutter i nærvær av den brennende busken. Neste morgen var han på vei tilbake til Egypt. Hans kone satt grense over et muldyr, med et barn på hver hofte. Moses, en mann på åtti år, gikk med en gammel stav i hånden og skjegget blafrende. "Hvor skal du, Moses?"
"Jeg skal til Egypt for å ta over." En enmanns invasjon. Og han gjorde det.
Hvorfor? Som en ung kriger hadde han en opplevelse, men han flyktet fra Farao uten en virkelig erfaring. Med en erfaring, derimot, konfronterte han Farao og sa: "Jeg vil slå dette landet med plager, med mindre du lar dette folket gå." Han var ikke redd. Hvorfor? Han hadde møtt Gud ansikt til ansikt.
Bror, søster, det er en hemmelig plass som enhver troende bør oppsøke: baksiden av ørkenen, der på den hellige sanden. Ingen teolog, gresk eller hebraisk lærd, ingen skole eller seminar… De kan forvirre deg med slike ting, men hvis du noen gang har møtt Gud på den hellige sanden, kan ikke Satan sette sine føtter der. Du vet at du har møtt Gud. Noe virkelig… Hva var det? Du snakket med Gud selv. Du hadde en erfaring.

text:
No man has the right to take the pulpit to preach the gospel, unless he's had a "backside of the desert" experience. No man, no person has the right to call themselves a Christian until they've had a "backside of the desert" experience. It's still the backside of the desert; we call it "Upper Room," but it's still the backside of the desert too. It's where you meet God.
Oh, how ridiculous it is tonight to take people in the church, just by baptizing into water and putting their name on the book. Oh, we need an old-fashioned, Saint Paul's revival, and the Bible Holy Ghost back into the church again, and a real old-fashioned meeting, an experience.
After you've had an experience, then you know what you're talking about. That's when you've got faith.
They might explain it and say, "Well now, you oughtn't to be a Methodist; you oughtn't to be Pentecostal." They might talk you out of that, but when it comes to a place for that sacred spot where you met God, brother, Satan can't put his dirty feet on that place. You know you've talked to Somebody there. You know something happened. Yes, sir. You were there. You know all about it. From then on, you can go and conduct yourself like a real Christian.
text_no:
Ingen har rett til å gå opp på prekestolen for å forkynne evangeliet, med mindre de har hatt en "opplevelse på baksiden av ørkenen." Ingen har rett til å kalle seg kristen før de har hatt en slik opplevelse. Dette refereres fortsatt som "baksiden av ørkenen"; vi kaller det "Øvre Rom," men det er fortsatt baksiden av ørkenen. Det er der du møter Gud.
Å nå ta mennesker inn i menigheten kun ved å døpe dem i vann og sette navnet deres i medlemsregisteret, er helt latterlig. Vi trenger en gammeldags Paulus-vekking, og Bibelens Hellige Ånd tilbake i menigheten igjen, og et skikkelig gammeldags møte, en ekte opplevelse.
Etter at du har hatt en slik opplevelse, vet du hva du snakker om. Det er da du har tro.
Noen kan kanskje forklare og si: "Du burde ikke være metodist; du burde ikke være pinsevenn." De kan prøve å overbevise deg om annet, men når det kommer til det hellige stedet hvor du møtte Gud, bror, kan ikke Satan sette sine skitne føtter der. Du vet at du har snakket med Noen der. Du vet at noe skjedde. Ja, helt riktig. Du var der. Du vet alt om det. Fra det øyeblikket kan du gå og opptre som en ekte kristen.

text:
Oh, the conduct today, of people calling themselves Christians… No wonder our poor women are painting their faces, and cutting off their hair, and wearing little shorts, and things. It's because they have failed to get that spot back there on that sacred sands yonder. That's right.
No wonder our men are permitting it, and smoking cigarettes, and things. They failed to find that sacred spot yonder, somewhere, where God and man meet together. That's right. That's what we need tonight. My beloved friend, you know that. It's a disgrace, on the days that we're living, that's the reason God cannot send a revival; He hasn't got much to build it on, friends.
text_no:
Å, oppførselen i dag blant folk som kaller seg kristne… Ikke rart at våre stakkars kvinner maler ansiktene sine, klipper håret og går i små shorts og lignende. Det er fordi de har unnlatt å finne det hellige stedet der borte. Det stemmer.
Ikke rart at våre menn tillater dette, røyker sigaretter og lignende. De har mislyktes i å finne det hellige stedet hvor Gud og mennesket møtes. Det er riktig. Det er hva vi trenger i kveld. Min kjære venn, du vet dette. Det er en skam i disse dager vi lever i; derfor kan ikke Gud sende en vekkelse. Han har ikke mye å bygge den på, venner.

text:
Yes, but I'm glad of this: "These stones are able to… God's able of these stones to raise children to Abraham." There's a few yet, left around it, in a shop and down at the works and so forth, that still love God. He's always had a remnant of people; He's got them tonight, that believe Him. No matter what anyone says, they believe it to be the truth: An experience, knowing God, knowing His power, loving Him. Oh, after you've had an experience with God, what a different person it makes you. How it makes you know where you're standing.
Even death itself can't separate you from it. No, David said, "Yea, though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death, I will fear no evil, for Thou art with me." Gets our conduct right.
text_no:
Ja, men jeg er glad for dette: "Gud er i stand til å reise barn for Abraham fra disse steinene." Det finnes fortsatt noen få igjen, både i butikkene og på arbeidsplassene, som fortsatt elsker Gud. Han har alltid hatt en rest av folk; Han har dem i kveld, som tror på Ham. Uansett hva noen sier, de tror det er sannheten: En opplevelse, å kjenne Gud, å kjenne Hans kraft, å elske Ham. Å, etter at du har hatt en opplevelse med Gud, hvilken forskjell det gjør i deg. Det får deg til å vite hvor du står.
Selv døden kan ikke skille deg fra det. David sa: "Ja, selv om jeg går gjennom dødsskyggens dal, frykter jeg ikke for noe ondt, for Du er med meg." Det får vår oppførsel til å bli riktig.

text:
Sometime ago, down in the South, they used to sell colored people for slaves, when the Africans brought them over here … the Boers and sold them for slaves. And they would buy them in markets just like you do used cars---human beings. And to sell them and buy them, and brokers would come by and do so.
One day, a broker came by a certain plantation to buy some slaves. And this owner said, "I've got a few slaves I'd sell."
He said, "Well, let me look them over." And he noticed the slaves; they were away from mama and papa, the baby and hubby, and they would never go back again. They were slaves, and they'd die here in a strange land. Many times they were sorry, and crying, and all disturbed, because they would never see their loved ones again. So they'd whip them to make them work.
text_no:
For en tid tilbake, i Sørstatene, pleide de å selge fargede mennesker som slaver. Afrikanerne ble brakt hit av boerne og solgt som slaver. De ble kjøpt og solgt på markeder, på samme måte som brukte biler—mennesker ble behandlet som varer. Mellommenn kom for å kjøpe og selge dem.
En dag kom en slik mellommann til en bestemt plantasje for å kjøpe slaver. Eieren sa: "Jeg har noen slaver jeg kan selge."
Mellommannen svarte: "La meg se på dem." Han la merke til at slavene var adskilt fra sine mødre, fedre, barn og ektefeller, og de ville aldri komme tilbake. De var slaver for evig, dømt til å leve og dø i et fremmed land. Mange av dem var triste, gråt og fortvilet, fordi de aldri ville se sine kjære igjen. For å få dem til å arbeide, ble de regelmessig pisket.

text:
And they happened to notice one young man. They didn't have to whip him. His head was back, and his chest out, and he was just right at the dock. And this slave buyer said, "I'd like to buy that slave."
But the owner said, "He's not for sale."
Said, "Why isn't he for sale?" Said, "Is he the boss over the rest of them?"
He said, "No. He's just a slave."
"Well," said, "perhaps maybe you feed him better than you feed the rest of the slaves?"
Said, "No, he eats out in the galley with the rest of them."
"Well," said, "what makes him so much different from the rest of them?"
He said, "I wondered a long time myself, till I found out. That boy's daddy, over in the homeland, is the king of the tribe. Though he's an alien and in a far country, yet he knows he's a king's son, and he conducts himself like one."
text_no:
De la merke til en ung mann. Han trengte ikke å piskes. Hodet var hevet, brystet frem, og han sto helt riktig ved brygga. Slavekjøperen sa, "Jeg vil kjøpe den slaven."
Men eieren svarte, "Han er ikke til salgs."
Kjøperen spurte, "Hvorfor er han ikke til salgs? Er han sjefen over de andre?"
"Nei," sa eieren, "han er bare en slave."
"Vel," sa kjøperen, "kanskje du gir ham bedre mat enn de andre?"
"Nei," svarte eieren, "han spiser ute i byssa med resten."
"Vel," fortsatte kjøperen, "hva gjør ham så annerledes fra de andre?"
Eieren svarte, "Jeg lurte lenge selv, til jeg fant ut av det. Gutten sin far er konge over stammen hjemme. Selv om han er i et fremmed land, vet han at han er en kongesønn, og han oppfører seg deretter."

text:
Oh, though we be criticized and called crazy, yet we're sons and daughters of the King. We're aliens, but yet we know that "Our Father is rich with houses and lands. He holdeth the wealth of the world in his hands." We're a child of the King. Let's conduct ourselves like one. Let's straighten ourselves up. Let's face the enemy and know that God is our Father. He's the King. Though we're in a world of fashion and education and theology; that has nothing to do with it. Stick your chest out. Walk forward. Believe God. Certainly. Have an experience when you're born in that royal family.
After you've had that experience, you conduct yourself like that. Certainly.
Now it was Philip, one time, when he went over to find Nathanael. He could go, do, and tell Nathanael after he'd heard Jesus say, "Your name is Simon. And your Father's name is Jonas." After he had had that experience, he could go tell Philip … or, Nathanael, what great things he'd saw Jesus do. What, before? After he had seen, after he had seen Jesus do that.
text_no:
Selv om vi blir kritisert og kalt gale, er vi likevel sønner og døtre av Kongen. Vi er fremmede, men vi vet at "Vår Far er rik på hus og land. Han holder verdens rikdom i Sine hender." Vi er barn av Kongen. La oss oppføre oss deretter. La oss rette oss opp og møte fienden med visshet om at Gud er vår Far. Han er Kongen. Selv om vi er i en verden av mote, utdanning og teologi, har det ingen betydning. Stram brystet, gå fremover og tro på Gud. Absolutt. Ha en opplevelse når du blir født inn i den kongelige familien. 
Når du har hatt den opplevelsen, oppfør deg deretter. Absolutt. 
Det var en gang da Filip dro for å finne Natanael. Han kunne gå og fortelle Natanael etter at han hadde hørt Jesus si: "Ditt navn er Simon, og din fars navn er Jonas." Etter at han hadde hatt den opplevelsen, kunne han gå og fortelle Natanael om de store tingene han hadde sett Jesus gjøre. Hva med før det? Etter at han hadde sett Jesus gjøre det.

text:
These apostles here… And before Jesus told them to have faith in God, and to move mountains, he showed what He could do. He cursed the tree and then let them see it withered. See, after they seen the tree wither, then He said, "Have faith in God."---after He had done something, after He had proved what He was.
And when Nathanael on his road back, and would been discussing with Philip, and saying, "Now nothing good could come out of Nazareth."
But when he walked up into the presence of Jesus, and Jesus said, "Behold, an Israelite, in whom there is no guile," he said, "Rabbi, when did you know me?"
He said, "Before Philip called you, when you were under the tree, I saw you."
It was after Jesus said that, that he said, "Thou art the Son of God. Thou art the King of Israel." After he had had an experience of seeing it, he knew then that He was the Son of God.
Jesus said, "Because I told you, I saw you under the tree, do you believe Me?"
text_no:
Disse apostlene her... Før Jesus ba dem ha tro på Gud og flytte fjell, viste Han hva Han kunne gjøre. Han forbannet treet og lot dem se det visne. Etter at de så treet visne, sa Han: "Ha tro på Gud." – etter at Han hadde gjort noe, etter at Han hadde bevist hvem Han var.
Da Natanael var på vei tilbake og diskuterte med Filip og sa: "Nå, ingenting godt kan komme fra Nasaret."
Men da han kom inn i Jesu nærvær, og Jesus sa: "Se, en ekte israelitt, i ham er det ingen svik," sa han: "Rabbi, hvordan kjente du meg?"
Jesus svarte: "Før Filip kalte deg, da du var under treet, så Jeg deg."
Etter at Jesus hadde sagt dette, sa han: "Du er Guds Sønn. Du er Israels Konge." Etter at han hadde hatt en opplevelse av å se det, visste han at Han var Guds Sønn.
Jesus sa, "Fordi Jeg sa, Jeg så deg under treet, tror du på Meg?"

text:
It was the woman that was fussing with Jesus about her religion and His religion, sitting at the well… "You say you're greater than our father, Jacob? Why, You're a Jew. There's segregation in this land. You ain't got no business talking to me. And You say people should worship at Jerusalem, and we say, 'in this mountain,'" See, she was fussing with Him about her religion. She wanted to hold a little debate with Him.
She didn't really realize who He was, till He said, "Go get your husband and come here."
She said, "I don't have any husband."
He said, "You've said the truth. You've had five. And the one you're living with now, is not your husband."
"Thou saidst right." It is after He said that, that the woman left her water pot and run into the city, and said, "Come, see a man who told me what I've done. Isn't this the Messiah?" After He had told her. She didn't want to argue with Him then. After He had made Hisself known…
text_no:
Det var kvinnen som diskuterte med Jesus om hennes religion og Hans religion, sittende ved brønnen. "Du sier at du er større enn vår far Jakob? Du er jo en jøde. Det er segregering i dette landet. Du har ikke noe med å snakke til meg. Og du sier folk bør tilbe i Jerusalem, mens vi sier 'på dette fjellet,'" Se, hun kranglet med Ham om hennes religion. Hun ønsket å holde en liten debatt med Ham.
Hun forsto egentlig ikke hvem Han var før Han sa, "Gå og hent din mann og kom hit."
Hun sa, "Jeg har ingen mann."
Han sa, "Du har sagt sannheten. Du har hatt fem menn, og den du lever med nå, er ikke din mann."
"Du sa riktig." Etter at Han sa det, forlot kvinnen vannkrukken og løp inn i byen og sa, "Kom, se en mann som har fortalt meg alt jeg har gjort. Er ikke dette Messias?" Etter at Han hadde fortalt henne, ville hun ikke diskutere med Ham lenger. Etter at Han hadde gjort Seg kjent...

text:
It was after the woman with the blood issue had touched His garment and went and sat down, believing in her heart that she had faith for her healing. And it was after Jesus looked around and said, "Who touched me?" and no one spoke. And He looked out into the audience and told the little woman what her trouble was, and she was healed. It was after that, that everybody, then, began to touch His garment, for they was testing their faith, too.
The world wants to see something real. I'm hungry for real things. I'm hungry to see a church stand in this last days. I'm hungry to see a good old-fashioned, Pentecostal body of Christ rise in the power of the Holy Ghost, calling right "right," and wrong "wrong." My heart longs to see it.
The little remnants that's waiting on, are not waiting to see how big we can make our denomination; they're not waiting to see how well we can dress or how big a church we can build; they're waiting to see us manifest the life of the Lord Jesus Christ in love and power.
text_no:
Etter at kvinnen med blodsykdommen hadde rørt ved Hans kappe og satt seg ned, overbevist i sitt hjerte om at hun hadde tro for sin helbredelse, så Jesus seg rundt og sa: "Hvem rørte ved Meg?" Ingen svarte. Han så ut over forsamlingen og fortalte den lille kvinnen hva hennes problem var, og hun ble helbredet. Etter dette begynte alle å røre ved Hans kappe, for de testet sin tro også.
Verden ønsker å se noe ekte. Jeg lengter etter virkelige ting. Jeg lengter etter å se en menighet stå fast i disse siste dager. Jeg lengter etter å se en god, gammeldags, pinsekristen Kristi kropp reise seg i Den Hellige Ånds kraft, som kaller rett for "rett" og galt for "galt." Mitt hjerte lengter etter å se det.
De små restene som venter, venter ikke på å se hvor stor vi kan gjøre vår konfesjon; de venter ikke på å se hvor godt vi kan kle oss eller hvor stor kirke vi kan bygge; de venter på å se oss manifestere livet til Herren Jesus Kristus i kjærlighet og kraft.

text:
They're waiting to see the time that when the Oneness, Twoness, Trinity and all together, will break down their little walls and put their hands in each other's hands and say, "We are brothers." It's waiting for the Assemblies, and the Holiness, Pentecostal Holiness, and the Church of God, for the Mexicans, and the colored people, and the Japanese, and the white, and all together, to join ranks as one great body of Jesus Christ.
They don't care how good we educate our preachers. They're wanting to see reality. Then they've got an experience if there is a reality. That's what we need: an experience.
text_no:
De venter på at Enhetsbevegelsen, Tospirasjon, Treenighetslærere og alle andre skal bryte ned sine små murer, rekke hverandre hendene og si: "Vi er brødre." De venter på at Forsamlingene, Hellighetsbevegelsen, Pinsebevegelsen, Guds Menighet, meksikanere, fargede, japanere og hvite skal forenes som én stor kropp av Jesus Kristus. 
De bryr seg ikke om hvor godt vi utdanner våre forkynnere. De ønsker å se virkeligheten. Da vil de ha en erfaring dersom det finnes en realitet. Det er hva vi trenger: en opplevelse.

text:
I seen the coldest hearted man I ever seen in my life brought to Christ by something like it, not long ago. Up here in these New England states where I hunt, I got a good friend. Think he's sitting right back here tonight. He was one of the best hunters I ever followed. And I love to hunt, no, not to kill the game. It's … just get alone with God, get away from all the gasoline, and cigarette smoke, and all the carrying on, so you could be alone with God, way up in the cathedral, in top of the mountain, miles and miles from nowhere. Then God comes down, and you can hear Him whispering through the pine. You can hear Him crying in the wolf, yonder. You can hear Him in the deer when he snorts. Why, you can just hear Him everywhere. As the eagle screams, you can watch Him, listen at Him.
And I used to hunt up here in the White Mountains with a good friend. He was a good hunter, a wonderful shot, and a nice fellow, but he was the most meanest man I ever seen. He was cruel-hearted. He used to shoot little fawns just make me feel bad. And he'd take little… A little fawn is a little baby deer.
text_no:
Jeg så den mest hjerteløse mannen jeg noensinne har møtt bli ført til Kristus gjennom noe lignende nylig. Her oppe i disse New England-statene hvor jeg jakter, har jeg en god venn. Jeg tror han sitter her i kveld. Han var en av de beste jegerne jeg noensinne har fulgt. Jeg elsker å jakte, ikke for å drepe viltet, men for å være alene med Gud. Bort fra alt av eksos, sigarettrøyk og støy, kan du være alene med Gud, høyt oppe i "katedralen" på toppen av fjellet, milevis fra alt. Da kommer Gud ned, og du kan høre Ham hviske gjennom furutrærne. Du kan høre Ham rope gjennom ulven. Du kan høre Ham i hjorten når den snøfter. Du kan høre Ham overalt. Når ørnen skriker, kan du se og lytte til Ham.
Jeg pleide å jakte her i White Mountains med en god venn. Han var en utmerket jeger, en fantastisk skytter og en hyggelig fyr, men han var også den mest grusomme mannen jeg noensinne har møtt. Han hadde et kaldt hjerte. Han pleide å skyte små rådyrkalver bare for å gjøre meg opprørt. En rådyrkalv er en liten babyhjort.

text:
And now, if the law says you can kill a fawn, that's all right; there's nothing against it. Abraham killed a calf and fed it to God; but not to just keep shooting them, just to be mean. See, it's your perversion, your attitude.
And so he would just shoot those little fawns. And I said, "Burt, don't do that. You're the meanest guy I ever seen."
And he said, "Oh, Billy, get next to yourself. You're just a chicken-hearted preacher."
"Why," I said, "it don't take a chicken-hearted preacher to know right from wrong." I said, "You shouldn't do that."
And one year, when I went up there, he'd had him a little whistle. And he could take that little whistle and go just like a little baby deer crying for it's mammy.
And I thought, "You're not going to use that, Burt."
"Oh," He said, "get next to yourself, Billy."
text_no:
Hvis loven tillater at man kan drepe et rådyr, er det greit; det er ikke noe galt med det. Abraham slaktet en kalv og serverte den til Gud; men ikke for å skyte dem bare for å være slem. Det handler om perversjonen din, holdningen din.
Han pleide å skyte disse små rådyrene. Jeg sa, "Burt, ikke gjør det. Du er den ondeste personen jeg noen gang har sett."
Han svarte, "Åh, Billy, skjerp deg. Du er bare en hønsehjertet forkynner."
Jeg sa, "Det krever ikke en hønsehjertet forkynner for å vite forskjellen på rett og galt. Du burde ikke gjøre det."
Et år, da jeg kom tilbake, hadde han fått seg en liten fløyte. Han kunne bruke den til å etterligne lyden av et rådyrkalv som roper etter sin mor.
Jeg tenkte, "Du kommer ikke til å bruke den, Burt."
Han sa, "Åh, skjerp deg, Billy."

text:
And we went hunting that day---a very fine hunter; I love to follow with him. And we'd separate in the woods, and you'd never have to think about hunting him up; you knowed where he was at. And so we was hunting till about noon together, and then we were going to separate and go back down different mountains to come back to our camp.
And it… Just before about eleven thirty, he was walking in front. We hadn't seen a track at all, and there was about a six inch snow on the ground, good tracking weather. But the deer season had been in for quite awhile and much shooting going on; the deers were all afraid. We call it "spooked." They were scared, stayed in the thickets, and under the brush piles, and wherever they could get, especially when the daylight was on.
text_no:
Vi dro på jakt den dagen. Han var en utmerket jeger; jeg elsker å følge ham. Vi skilte lag i skogen, og du trengte aldri bekymre deg for å lete etter ham; du visste alltid hvor han var. Vi jaktet sammen til omtrent midt på dagen, og deretter skulle vi gå hver vår vei ned forskjellige fjellsider, for så å møtes igjen ved leiren.
Rundt klokken halv tolv gikk han foran meg. Vi hadde ikke sett noen spor hele dagen, til tross for at det lå omtrent femten centimeter snø på bakken, som vanligvis er godt for sporing. Jaktsesongen for hjort hadde vart en stund, og det hadde vært mye skyting; hjortene var redde. Vi kalte dem "spooket." De holdt seg i kratt, under buskhauger og hvor enn de kunne finne skjul, spesielt når det var lys ute.

text:
So, he kind of stooped down at a little opening, and the first thing you know I seen him reach into his coat, and I thought he was going to get his lunch and we'd have our lunch together. We'd packed hot chocolate and some sandwiches, where if we got lost… And then he reached in there and pulled out this little old whistle, and he gave it a little cry. And when he did, just across the opening, about the distance of this place, a mother doe stood up. Now, that's the mother deer: the doe. Oh, she was a beautiful animal. And I will never forget how she looked. She raised up those great big beautiful ears, standing up, her big brown eyes, and the veins looked like in her face, you could just see them.
And he looked around to me with that lizard looking eyes and he said… Kind of pulled himself down. I thought, "Burt, you won't do that." And I heard the lever come back, and he pulled the bolt back, and throwed a shell up into that 30.06 rifle. I thought, "Burt, surely you won't." So he squealed again like that, and the old mother deer… Now, that's very unusual for them to raise up like that, especially that time of day, in hunting season. She walked right out into the opening.
text_no:
Han bøyde seg ned ved en liten åpning, og det neste jeg så var at han rakte hånden ned i frakken. Jeg trodde han skulle ta ut maten sin slik at vi kunne spise lunsj sammen. Vi hadde pakket varm sjokolade og noen smørbrød, i tilfelle vi gikk oss bort. Men så tok han frem en liten gammel fløyte og ga den en liten pip. Idet han gjorde det, reiste det seg en hjort, bare et stykke unna åpningen. Det var ei hind, en vakker skapning. Jeg vil aldri glemme hvordan hun så ut. Hun reiste de store, vakre ørene sine, og de store, brune øynene og årene i ansiktet hennes var tydelige.
Han så på meg med de blikkende øynene og sa med en lav stemme: "Burt, du vil vel ikke gjøre det?" Jeg hørte han trekke hanen tilbake og lade rifla. Jeg tenkte: "Burt, du kan ikke mene det." Han blåste i fløyta igjen, og den gamle hinden... Det er veldig uvanlig for dem å reise seg opp sånn, spesielt på den tiden av dagen, i jaktsesongen. Hun gikk rett ut i åpningen.

text:
Now, any of you brethren that hunts know they won't do that. And it's so… What was it, though? What was she standing there? And Burt moved. And the deer saw him. Did she run? No, she stood there. What was it? She wasn't playing church. She was a mother. There was something in her was real. It was real love. A baby was crying, and she had something in her that called and answered; she was a mother. And she seen the hunter; she quivered a few times, and she looked again for that baby.
I seen Burt level that rifle down; that scope hair come right across her heart. I thought, "Oh!" He was a dead shot. I thought, "Oh, my, in another second or two he will blow her loyal heart plumb through her." I thought, "How can you do it, Burt? How can you be so mean?" That mother out there, displaying real mother love. She isn't a hypocrite. She's really… There's a baby in trouble. Death or no death---she's a mother.
text_no:
Enhver av dere brødre som jakter, vet at dette ikke er normalt. Men hva var det egentlig? Hva gjorde hun der? Og Burt beveget seg. Rådyret så ham. Løp hun? Nei, hun stod stille. Hvorfor? Hun spilte ikke menighet. Hun var en mor. Det var noe ekte i henne. Det var virkelig kjærlighet. En baby gråt, og hun hadde noe i seg som kalte og svarte; hun var en mor. Hun så jegeren; hun skalv noen ganger, og hun så igjen etter babyen.
Jeg så Burt rette riflen mot henne; korset i siktet kom rett over hjertet hennes. Jeg tenkte: "Å!" Han var en skarpskytter. Jeg tenkte: "Å, nei, om et sekund eller to vil han blåse hennes lojale hjerte rett gjennom henne." Jeg tenkte: "Hvordan kan du gjøre det, Burt? Hvordan kan du være så ond?" Den moren der ute, som viser ekte moderlig kjærlighet. Hun er ingen hykler. Hun er virkelig… Det er en baby i nød. Død eller ei—hun er en mor.

text:
I thought, "How can you do it?" That big hundred and eighty grain bullet, that close, would blow her fifteen feet and blow her heart plumb through her. I thought, "Burt, how can you do that? You cruel-hearted man, shoot that poor mother's heart right out of her, with a heart like that, so loyal. So loyal."
I turned my back. I thought, "O God, have mercy on that man. How can he be so cruel?" I waited, waited. The gun never fired. And I turned to look, and the gun was going like this… He looked around at me, the tears running down his cheeks, he threw the gun on the ground. He said, "Billy, I've had enough of it. Tell me about that Jesus." There on that bank of snow, I lead that cruel-hearted man to Christ. Why? Not my preaching, no, but because he seen something real. He seen something that was not put on. He seen something genuine.
text_no:
Jeg tenkte, "Hvordan kan du gjøre det?" Den store kulen på hundre og åtti grain ville på så nært hold blåse henne femten fot av gårde og sprenge hjertet hennes rett ut. Jeg tenkte, "Burt, hvordan kan du gjøre det? Du grusomme mann, skyte hjertet ut av den stakkars moren, med et så lojalt hjerte. Så lojalt."
Jeg snudde ryggen til. Jeg tenkte, "Å Gud, ha miskunn med den mannen. Hvordan kan han være så grusom?" Jeg ventet, og ventet. Skuddet kom aldri. Jeg snudde meg for å se, og våpenet ristet i hendene hans. Han så på meg, med tårer rennende nedover kinnene, kastet våpenet på bakken og sa, "Billy, jeg har fått nok. Fortell meg om Jesus." Der på snøbanken ledet jeg den grusomme mannen til Kristus. Hvorfor? Ikke på grunn av min forkynnelse, men fordi han så noe ekte. Han så noe som ikke var tilgjort. Han så noe genuint.

text:
I'm telling you tonight, friends, the world is looking for something genuine, not a manmade creed, but a Jesus who lives, and reigns, and brings love, and peace, and joy, and breaks down differences, and makes men love men, and women love women. The world wants to see something real.
Brother, Sister, God's got it for you. It's here right now. Just meet that real thing. Just become as much Christian as she was a mother. I don't care; they can call you holy-roller; they can call you fanatic; you'll stand in the face of the enemy and declare that you love God. Real faith: think of it. That's what we need tonight, friends, is something real.
text_no:
I kveld, venner, forteller jeg dere at verden leter etter noe ekte. Ikke noe menneskeskapt trosbekjennelse, men en levende Jesus som hersker, bringer kjærlighet, fred og glede, bryter ned forskjeller og får mennesker til å elske hverandre. Verden ønsker å se noe autentisk.
Bror, Søster, Gud har det for dere. Det er her akkurat nå. Bare møt det ekte. Bli like mye en kristen som hun var en mor. Jeg bryr meg ikke om folk kaller dere fanatikere eller hellige-rullere; dere vil stå ansikt til ansikt med fienden og erklære at dere elsker Gud. Ekte tro: tenk på det. Det er det vi trenger i kveld, venner—noe ekte.

text:
Let us bow our heads just a moment now.
